attacking_style: Launches disruptive attacks on final climb approaches and from breakaways, targeting moments that force GC teams to react. Demonstrates strong climbing ability to sustain efforts against higher-ranked opposition.
team_tactics: Used in dual-threat breakaway role: either as leadout support for teammates in intermediate sprints or as protected attacker when breakaway survival is viable. Team grants freedom for opportunistic moves.
terrain_preference: Climbing stages where breakaways have survival potential. Effective on approaches to final climbs where attacks can create tactical disruption. Strong enough to resist GC team pressure in breakaways.
vulnerability: Limited data on head-to-head GC competition. Role appears constrained to breakaway hunting rather than sustained stage racing—no evidence of multi-day defensive riding or GC ambitions.
key_patterns: Attacks on final climb approaches to force reactions, evidenced in 2 races
Breakaway specialist capable of finishing podium against GC team pressure
Versatile tactical usage: both support role and protected attacker in breakaways
Strong climbing credentials demonstrated across Dauphiné and Tour stages
form_trajectory: positive: Stepped up from reconnaissance/support role early Tour to breakaway podium finish, showing development within Grand Tour
